title: Road resurfacing and pothole repair — Scheme 54 corridor, Vijay Nagar
feasibility_evidence: Est. ₹95L for 2.4 km resurfacing; IMC road-maintenance head available; monsoon window limits work to patch-first, full relay post-rains (5-month timeline).

title: Primary school building repair and girls' toilet block — Banganga Ward
feasibility_evidence: Est. ₹18L, 3-month timeline; existing government building and land, MPLAD head applicable; UDISE+ flags 22% ward dropout vs 5-6% in planned wards — repair directly addresses documented dropout driver (closed classrooms, no girls' toilet).

title: New vocational training centre — Banganga Ward
feasibility_evidence: Est. ₹1.2Cr; requires land acquisition and new construction (12-18 months); no trainer staffing sanction yet; complaint data points to primary-school infrastructure, not skilling, as the active dropout driver.

title: LED streetlight installation and dark-spot elimination — Banganga main road and inner lanes
feasibility_evidence: Est. ₹9L for 140 LED fixtures on existing poles; 6-week timeline under IMC smart-lighting rate contract; ward streetlight density already mapped 'low' — quick win with direct night-safety co-benefit.

title: Narmada pipeline replacement — AB Road / Geeta Bhawan stretch, Palasia
feasibility_evidence: Est. ₹68L for 800m line replacement; requires road cutting permission and night work on arterial AB Road; 4-month timeline; also arrests ongoing road-base erosion at the same spot.

title: Sewer line desilting and drain reconstruction — Musakhedi main nala
feasibility_evidence: Est. ₹42L; desilting immediate (3 weeks) but drain reconstruction needs AMRUT 2.0 sanction (files pending at UADD); phased delivery possible; reduces the stagnant water driving the ward's dengue cluster.

title: Health sub-centre upgrade with daily OPD — Musakhedi
feasibility_evidence: Est. ₹1.6Cr; needs NHM staffing sanction for one MO and two ANMs plus building expansion on disputed plot; 18-24 month realistic timeline; ward phc_per_10k at 0.3 vs 0.9 in planned wards documents the gap.
